name: clawchrome-cli parity with chrome-devtools-axi
description: Bring the supported clawchrome-cli command surface into explicit behavioral parity with chrome-devtools-axi while preserving a canonical Go implementation and excluding intentionally unsupported APIs from the runtime surface.

userStories.title: Match pages command output to the JS implementation
userStories.description: As a CLI user, I want `clawchrome-cli pages` to render the same structured page listing shape as `chrome-devtools-axi` so that page management output is predictable across implementations.
acceptanceCriteria: `clawchrome-cli pages` parses the `list_pages` MCP output instead of returning the raw tool response.
`clawchrome-cli pages` renders the same structured `pages[n]{id,url,selected}` table shape used by `chrome-devtools-axi`.
The help text appended to `pages` output matches the intent and structure of the `chrome-devtools-axi` implementation.
The implementation is written idiomatically in Go and does not shell out to the JS implementation.
`mise run test` passes
`mise run build` passes

userStories.title: Match closepage behavior and guardrails to the JS implementation
userStories.description: As a CLI user, I want `clawchrome-cli closepage` to enforce the same last-page safety behavior as `chrome-devtools-axi` so that tab management works the same way across implementations.
acceptanceCriteria: `clawchrome-cli closepage <id>` checks page count before closing a tab.
When only one page is open, `clawchrome-cli closepage <id>` returns a no-op status and help text matching the `chrome-devtools-axi` behavior rather than attempting the close.
When multiple pages are open, `clawchrome-cli closepage <id>` closes the target page and returns the same structured status shape as `chrome-devtools-axi`.
Validation errors for missing or invalid page IDs match the intent and clarity of the `chrome-devtools-axi` implementation.
The implementation is written idiomatically in Go and does not shell out to the JS implementation.
`mise run test` passes
`mise run build` passes

userStories.title: Normalize wait command validation, suggestions, and output
userStories.description: As a CLI user, I want `clawchrome-cli wait` to match the JS CLI's validation messaging, help text, and output structure so that waiting behavior is consistent across implementations.
acceptanceCriteria: `clawchrome-cli wait` emits validation errors for missing targets with the same intent, specificity, and examples as `chrome-devtools-axi`.
`clawchrome-cli wait <milliseconds>` and `clawchrome-cli wait <text>` produce the same output block structure and suggestion behavior as `chrome-devtools-axi` for the supported command surface.
The wait command help text and follow-up suggestions in Go match the behavior of the JS implementation rather than using a simplified local variant.
Any remaining internal JS-evaluation dependency for numeric wait is explicitly isolated and documented only where unavoidable until a native API replacement exists.
The implementation is written idiomatically in Go and does not shell out to the JS implementation.
`mise run test` passes
`mise run build` passes

userStories.title: Bring supported command argument parsing into parity with the JS CLI
userStories.description: As a CLI user, I want supported `clawchrome-cli` commands to accept and validate arguments the same way as `chrome-devtools-axi` so that command usage is predictable across implementations.
acceptanceCriteria: For every supported command in `clawchrome-cli`, argument parsing, `--full` handling, validation messages, and accepted option forms are audited against `chrome-devtools-axi`.
Any supported command whose parsing is currently simpler in Go is updated to match the JS implementation's behavior for the same command surface.
Unsupported APIs and commands remain absent from the `clawchrome-cli` runtime surface and are not reintroduced as part of the parity work.
The implementation is written idiomatically in Go and does not shell out to the JS implementation.
`mise run test` passes
`mise run build` passes

userStories.title: Bring help, suggestions, and TOON output formatting into parity
userStories.description: As a CLI user, I want `clawchrome-cli` output, help, and next-step suggestions to match `chrome-devtools-axi` for the supported commands so that the Go CLI feels like a direct translation rather than a simplified fork.
acceptanceCriteria: The Go implementation audits `internal/cli/cli.go`, `internal/suggestions/suggestions.go`, and related helpers against `chrome-devtools-axi/src/cli.ts` for every supported command.
For supported commands, the TOON block ordering, field naming, truncation behavior, and help rendering match the `chrome-devtools-axi` implementation.
Suggestion generation in Go matches the intent and coverage of the JS implementation for the supported command surface rather than using a lighter-weight subset.
The implementation remains canonical Go code and does not embed or proxy the JS formatter.
`mise run test` passes
`mise run build` passes

userStories.title: Add supported-surface parity tests against the JS CLI contract
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want automated tests that pin supported-surface parity between `clawchrome-cli` and `chrome-devtools-axi` so that future changes do not silently drift from the intended Go translation.
acceptanceCriteria: Add table-driven Go tests that cover the supported command surface for argument parsing, validation errors, output shape, and help text parity expectations.
Tests explicitly cover the previously identified parity gaps: `pages`, `closepage`, `wait`, supported command option handling, suggestions, and TOON output formatting.
The tests verify parity against the behavior contract defined by `chrome-devtools-axi`, while remaining fully implemented in Go.
Unsupported APIs and commands are not included as runtime support targets in the parity suite.
`mise run test` passes
`mise run build` passes

userStories.title: Replace scroll and numeric wait JS evaluation with native tool calls
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want the remaining `scroll` and numeric `wait` TODO paths in `clawchrome-cli` to stop relying on `evaluate_script` so that the Go CLI no longer depends on JS execution for those supported behaviors.
acceptanceCriteria: The `scroll` command no longer calls `evaluate_script` and instead uses a native MCP tool path or an equivalent canonical non-JS implementation.
The numeric `wait` path no longer calls `evaluate_script` and instead uses a native MCP tool path or an equivalent canonical non-JS implementation.
The TODO comments in `internal/cli/cli.go` for `scroll` and numeric `wait` are removed because the native replacement is complete.
The resulting behavior for supported usage remains compatible with the intended `clawchrome-cli` command contract after the native migration.
The implementation is written idiomatically in Go and does not shell out to the JS implementation.
`mise run test` passes
`mise run build` passes
